# Jay Pokale

> Engineer & graduate researcher at IIT Hyderabad. Top 1% competitive programmer
> worldwide. Founder of Dare2Solve, a 20,000-strong mathematics community.
> Site: https://jaypokale.vercel.app · Contact: jay.pokale.35@gmail.com

## Facts

- LeetCode: Guardian badge, peak contest rating 2285 (top 0.94% of 874,830),
  1,637 problems solved (341 Hard) — https://leetcode.com/u/jaypokale
- Codeforces: Expert, rating 1770, handle rdx_panther —
  https://codeforces.com/profile/rdx_panther
- Research (IIT Hyderabad, two theses in progress):
  1. "Certifiably Robust GraphRAG for Multi-Hop Fraud Reasoning" — certified
     robustness guarantees for graph-based retrieval-augmented generation
     under retrieval corruption.
  2. "The Locality–Integrity Law" — bounds on keyed cache pollution: attacker
     cost vs. damage before detection.
- Selected works:
  - RDXmin — token-efficiency toolkit for AI coding agents; halved measured
    output bills; ships as a Claude Code plugin. https://github.com/JayPokale/RDXmin
  - GST Legal RAG — production retrieval system over Indian GST law with an
    LLM-judged eval harness, exact-arithmetic routing, crash-resumable
    ingestion, and abstention over fabrication.
  - typed-numarray — npm package for dynamic typed arrays in JavaScript.
    https://www.npmjs.com/package/typed-numarray
  - competitive — a documented DSA library for competitive programming in
    JavaScript. https://github.com/JayPokale/competitive
- Community: founder of Dare2Solve (20k+ members), mathematics problem-solving.
- Profiles: https://github.com/JayPokale · https://www.linkedin.com/in/JayPokale
  · https://x.com/JayPokale35

## Note

The website presents this as "Problem J." — a competitive-programming problem
statement whose editorial is Jay's career. Verdict: Accepted.
